name: Serra de São Tiago Loop
name_original: De Lousada até a Serra de São Tiago — percurso circular
description: This 9.0-mile (14.5 km) moderate touring cycling route from Lousada to Serra de São Tiago offers paved surfaces and scenic views.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Boim are characterized by varied terrain, including rolling hills and rural landscapes. The region features routes with notable elevation changes, offering cyclists a range of challenges. Routes often traverse areas with a mix of agricultural land and scattered woodlands, providing diverse scenery. The topography around Boim is generally hilly, with several routes including significant ascents. Best touring cycling routes around Boim The most popular touring cycling route is Serra de São Tiago Loop, a moderate 9.0 miles (14.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete. This route features rolling terrain with notable elevation changes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel – Alto do Pico loop from Lousada, a moderate 16.6 miles (26.7 km) path. It offers varied landscapes, including ascents to viewpoints. Local touring cyclists also love the Ponte de Espindo – Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel loop from Lousada, a 20.2 miles (32.6 km) trail leading through rural areas with significant climbs, often completed in about 2 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Boim is defined by rural roads, varied elevations, and open landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more difficult routes with substantial climbs. The routes in Boim are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.88 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 280 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Boim's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Boim?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes available around Boim, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. These routes are generally characterized by varied terrain and notable elevation changes.

Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling around Boim?
A: Touring cycling routes around Boim feature varied terrain, including rolling hills and rural landscapes. You can expect a mix of agricultural land and scattered woodlands, with the topography generally being hilly and including significant ascents on many routes.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes for beginners around Boim?
A: While the region offers a range of challenges, there are no routes specifically categorized as 'easy' for touring cycling around Boim. Most routes are rated as moderate or difficult, featuring notable elevation changes and requiring a reasonable level of fitness.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in Boim?
A: The touring cycling routes around Boim are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.88 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 280 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the area, often praising the varied terrain and the challenge of the climbs.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Boim?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Boim are designed as loops. For example, you can try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1380580457/serra-de-sao-tiago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de São Tiago Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.0-mile (14.5 km) trail, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10410903" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel – Ponte de Espindo loop from Lousada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting places can I explore along touring cycling routes near Boim?
A: While cycling around Boim, you can discover several interesting landmarks. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6301421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on several routes, or exploring the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/884029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint Peter of Ferreira&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/770851" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedra do Monte de Marecos&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes with significant climbs or viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The region around Boim is known for its hilly topography. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32208003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel – Alto do Pico loop from Lousada&lt;/a&gt; offer varied landscapes, including ascents to viewpoints, providing rewarding panoramas of the surrounding rural areas.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes around Boim?
A: Given the generally hilly terrain and the absence of 'easy' rated routes, most touring cycling options around Boim are better suited for cyclists with some experience. Families with younger children or those seeking very flat, easy rides might find the routes challenging.

Q: What are some longer touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can tackle routes with significant climbs and longer distances. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23745091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte de Espindo – Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel loop from Lousada&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 20.2-mile (32.6 km) trail leading through rural areas with substantial climbs, often completed in about 2 hours 23 minutes.

Q: Is public transport accessible for touring cyclists in the Boim area?
A: Information regarding specific public transport options directly serving touring cycling routes in Boim is limited. For routes starting from towns like Lousada, local bus services might be available, but it's advisable to check local transport schedules and bicycle carriage policies in advance.

Q: Are there dog-friendly touring cycling routes around Boim?
A: While there are no specific 'dog-friendly' designations for touring cycling routes, many rural roads and trails around Boim are open to cyclists. If you plan to bring your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, on a leash where necessary, and that you carry enough water for both of you, especially on longer rides with climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling around Boim?
A: The region's climate generally allows for touring cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures, making them ideal for tackling the hilly terrain. Summers can be warm, while winters are typically mild but may bring more rain.

In an economically very developed area but with a strong agricultural tradition, Lousada also has a monumental heritage that deserves to be visited.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time for this route?
faqs.answer: The estimated cycling time for the From Lousada to Serra de São Tiago circular route is approximately 1 hour and 5 minutes. This duration is based on an average touring cyclist's pace and can vary depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or less experienced riders?
faqs.answer: While Komoot rates this route as 'moderate' and suggests 'good fitness' due to its 350 meters of elevation gain, it is also noted as suitable for 'all skill levels' because of its mostly paved surfaces. This means that while there are climbs, the technical difficulty of the terrain is not high, making it accessible for a range of cyclists, including those with less experience who are comfortable with some uphill sections.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the start of the route?
faqs.answer: Specific parking details for the start of this particular route are not provided in the available information. However, as it's a circular route starting from Lousada, you would typically find parking options within or near the town center of Lousada to begin your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can expect primarily paved surfaces along this route, making it suitable for touring bicycles. Despite the 'Serra' (mountain range) in its name, the technical difficulty is low, though you will encounter hilly sections with an elevation gain of about 350 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the area around Lousada and into the Serra de São Tiago. You can expect hilly or mountainous landscapes characteristic of a 'serra,' likely offering natural beauty and potentially panoramic views from higher elevations. While specific details are not extensively provided, cycling routes in this region of Portugal often feature diverse landscapes.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route includes a visit to the Serra de São Tiago, which is a designated viewpoint, suggesting scenic vistas. You will also pass by the Senhor dos Aflitos Chapel, a religious building of interest.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with any other cycling or walking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with Caminho do Românico 1, Caminho do Românico 2, Trilho de Louredo da Serra, and the path leading to Ponte românica de Nevogilde.

faqs.question: Is the From Lousada to Serra de São Tiago circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding whether this specific cycling route is dog-friendly is not available. As it's primarily a cycling route on paved surfaces, dogs would typically need to be able to keep pace or be carried in a suitable bicycle trailer if permitted.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to cycle the From Lousada to Serra de São Tiago circular route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the From Lousada to Serra de São Tiago route?
faqs.answer: The best time of year to cycle this route would generally be during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) when the weather in Portugal is typically milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities. Summers can be quite hot, and winters might bring more rain.

faqs.question: What gear is recommended for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: Given that it's a touring bicycle route with moderate difficulty and some elevation gain, it's recommended to have a well-maintained touring bicycle, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, water, and basic repair tools. As the route involves climbs, comfortable gearing will be beneficial.
